arabic_text:
لَا يَكْمُلُ الْإِيمَانُ فِي قَلْبِ امْرِئٍ — حَتَّى تُحِيطَ بِهِ حُدُودٌ أَرْبَعٌ
مِنْ أَيْنَ جَاءَ الْإِنْسَانُ، ثُمَّ مَعَادُهُ — وَلِمَاذَا جَاءَ، وَلِمَاذَا يَرْجِعُ
alt_text: Arabic two-row pedagogical table: 'Faith is not complete in the heart of a person until four boundaries encompass it — from where did the human come, then where is his return, why did he come, and why does he return.'
notes: Traditional Ismaili teaching maxim about the four existential questions that must be answered for faith to be complete.
